question_text: A car of mass 1000 kg is travelling at 10 m/s. Calculate its kinetic energy using the formula KE = ½mv².
explanation: Using KE = ½mv², we substitute m = 1000 kg and v = 10 m/s. KE = ½ × 1000 × 10² = ½ × 1000 × 100 = 50,000 J. Always remember to square the velocity and divide the mass by 2.
